extra_fields.detailed_description: Gather historical shipping delay data for Nuuk Harbour from 2018 to 2025, including ice blockage frequency, duration, and average customs clearance times. Use the Danish Maritime Authority (DMA) API to extract logs and compile a dataset for risk analysis.

Use Pandas and Matplotlib in Python to analyze collected shipping data, map delay patterns by month, and simulate the probability of >4-week delays due to storms or ice. Validate results against Greenlandic Port Authority reports.
resources_needed: Python environment with Pandas and Matplotlib

extra_fields.detailed_description: Engage a supply chain analyst specializing in Arctic logistics to review the risk model. Submit findings to the Danish Maritime Authority for cross-verification and present results to the Inuit Cultural Heritage Council for cultural context on shipping disruptions.

Simulate emergency air freight response time using Nordic Cargo’s transit data (72-hour max). Confirm integration capability with real-time Arctic shipping alerts and document contingency protocols for rapid activation.

Compile all findings into a validated Arctic shipping risk assessment report by March 31, 2026. Include a 90% confidence interval for winter delay probabilities and recommendations for biannual import scheduling aligned with navigation windows.

task_children.description: Finalize Quarterly Clay Shipment Contract with Danish Supplier

extra_fields.detailed_description: Negotiate and sign a quarterly clay shipment contract with the Danish supplier, ensuring terms include weather-related delay clauses, delivery windows, and compliance with Greenlandic import regulations. This includes preparing bilingual (Danish/Greenlandic) documentation, conducting legal review, and securing approval from the project manager and financial analyst.

Review and confirm all contract terms with the Danish supplier, including pricing stability, minimum order volumes, and contingency plans for Arctic shipping disruptions. Conduct a joint verification session with the supply chain coordinator and logistics lead to ensure alignment with risk mitigation strategies.

Prepare and finalize all contract documents in both Danish and Greenlandic, ensuring legal accuracy and cultural appropriateness. Submit to the advisory council for feedback and obtain formal sign-off before execution.

Conduct the official signing of the clay shipment agreement with the Danish supplier, recording the event with digital documentation and distributing signed copies to all stakeholders. Confirm that the contract is stored in the central compliance repository with version control.

task_children.description: Secure Emergency Air Freight Agreement with Nordic Cargo

extra_fields.detailed_description: Finalize a pre-negotiated agreement with Nordic Cargo for emergency air freight during high-risk winter months. This includes confirming guaranteed delivery slots, defining trigger conditions for activation (e.g., >4-week shipping delay), and integrating the service into the real-time monitoring system. Ensure all terms are aligned with the project’s risk mitigation strategy and budget.

Procure and store a 50 kg buffer stock of clay in advance of the first shipment. Develop a protocol for tracking inventory levels, triggering reorders, and managing usage during supply disruptions. Coordinate with the Danish supplier and Nordic Cargo to ensure seamless replenishment if needed.

Deploy and configure IoT sensors and data integration tools to monitor Arctic shipping alerts in real time. Set up automated alerts based on predefined thresholds (e.g., ice blockage, storm warnings) and link them to the digital shift calendar and emergency response protocols. Conduct a dry-run simulation to validate system responsiveness.

task_children.description: Establish Buffer Stock of 50 kg Clay

extra_fields.detailed_description: Procure and store a 50 kg buffer stock of clay to mitigate supply chain disruptions. This includes coordinating with the Danish supplier for pre-shipment, ensuring customs documentation is ready, and verifying delivery timelines. The buffer must be on-site by July 15, 2026, to support the soft launch.

Confirm that all import documentation for clay shipments is prepared in both Danish and Greenlandic, and validate clearance times at Nuuk Port using historical data and port authority reports. Establish a real-time tracking protocol to flag delays exceeding 48 hours.

Deploy and test the IoT-based shipping alert system using the Danish Maritime Authority API and Nordic Cargo’s transit data. Ensure alerts are visible via the digital dashboard and trigger automated responses when delays exceed 72 hours.

task_children.description: Integrate Real-Time Shipping Alert Monitoring System

extra_fields.detailed_description: Finalize and lock in a dedicated emergency air freight agreement with Nordic Cargo for high-risk winter periods, ensuring rapid response to Arctic shipping disruptions. This includes confirming transit times (max 72 hours), pricing, and capacity guarantees. The agreement must be signed and active before any clay shipments are scheduled.

Procure and store a 50 kg buffer stock of clay to bridge potential supply chain gaps caused by ice blockages, storms, or customs delays. This involves coordinating with the Danish supplier for pre-shipment delivery, verifying quality, and securing storage in climate-controlled conditions within the workshop space.

Deploy and integrate an IoT-based real-time shipping alert monitoring system using data from the Danish Maritime Authority API. Configure automated alerts for delays exceeding 48 hours, and ensure all relevant staff receive notifications via mobile and digital dashboards. Conduct a test simulation to validate system responsiveness.

task_children.description: Conduct Winter Thermal Load Simulation Using EnergyPlus

extra_fields.detailed_description: Conduct a dynamic thermal load simulation using EnergyPlus software with real-world boundary conditions, including Nuuk's extreme winter temperatures (-20°C), 90% occupancy levels, and peak kiln operation. Input verified Greenlandic meteorological data from the Danish Meteorological Institute to model temperature gradients, humidity, CO₂ buildup, and energy consumption across drying zones and workspaces. Validate results against physical sensor readings from a mock workshop setup in December 2025.
resources_needed: Mock workshop setup with temporary heating systems

extra_fields.detailed_description: Perform statistical regression analysis to compare simulation outputs from EnergyPlus with real-time sensor data collected during the December 2025 mock test. Identify discrepancies in thermal stability, humidity control, and energy usage. Adjust input parameters as needed and confirm that all zones remain within safe operating parameters (±5°C variance) under 90% winter occupancy. Document findings for inclusion in the Winter Thermal Readiness Report.

Engage an Arctic-certified building engineer to audit the EnergyPlus model and final thermal assessment. Review the simulation methodology, input assumptions, and output reliability. Obtain formal sign-off confirming the facility’s thermal readiness for year-round operation. Submit the signed report to the National Labour Inspectorate (Greenland) for compliance review and ensure alignment with NFPA and Bygningsreglementet standards.

task_children.description: Install Passive Solar Design Elements (South-Facing Windows, Thermal Mass Walls)

extra_fields.detailed_description: Procure south-facing windows and thermal mass wall materials from Danish suppliers with guaranteed delivery timelines. Confirm shipping schedules with Nordic Cargo to ensure on-time arrival during the favorable weather window (late spring/early summer). Prioritize pre-fabricated, modular components to minimize on-site installation time.

Coordinate with certified Arctic contractors to schedule installation during the optimal weather period (late spring/early summer) when snow and ice are minimal. Include a 5-day buffer for potential weather-related delays and confirm site readiness through pre-installation inspections.

Perform thorough site assessments before installation begins to verify structural integrity, foundation stability, and alignment with design plans. Document any discrepancies and resolve them prior to material delivery to prevent rework or delays.

task_children.description: Install solar elements and thermal walls

extra_fields.detailed_description: Execute the physical installation of south-facing windows and thermal mass walls according to the approved design. Ensure all work complies with Arctic building standards and is documented for compliance audits. Conduct a post-installation quality review with the safety officer.

After installation, conduct a formal verification check against the project’s thermal performance goals and building code requirements. Record all installation details, photos, and test results in the digital compliance log for future audit purposes.

task_children.description: Deploy IoT Environmental Monitoring Sensors (Temp, Humidity, CO₂)

extra_fields.detailed_description: Install temperature, humidity, and CO₂ sensors in the workshop space to monitor environmental conditions. Ensure all sensors are calibrated and connected to the central data platform. Conduct a test run for 48 hours to verify real-time data transmission and alert thresholds.
resources_needed: Network connectivity hardware (Wi-Fi access points or cellular modems)
Data integration platform (e.g., Airtable, custom dashboard)
On-site technician with IoT setup experience

extra_fields.detailed_description: Validate that all sensors are correctly transmitting data to the monitoring system. Confirm that alerts trigger at predefined thresholds (e.g., high CO₂, extreme humidity). Perform a simulated failure test to ensure system resilience and response protocols.

Conduct hands-on training sessions for all staff and instructors on interpreting sensor data, responding to alerts, and using the dashboard during daily operations. Provide bilingual (Danish/Greenlandic) user guides and quick-reference cards.

task_children.description: Complete Pre-Fit-Out Thermal Audit and Safety Training Rollout

extra_fields.detailed_description: Perform a comprehensive pre-fit-out thermal audit of the workshop space using verified Arctic climate data and input from a certified Arctic building specialist. The audit will assess heat retention, insulation effectiveness, and identify potential weaknesses in the passive solar design under extreme winter conditions. Results will inform necessary upgrades to insulation and heating systems before fit-out begins.
resources_needed: Historical weather data from Danish Meteorological Institute
Digital documentation platform for audit reports

extra_fields.detailed_description: Develop and deliver mandatory safety training for all staff and future instructors, covering fire safety, kiln operation, material handling, emergency procedures, and compliance with Greenlandic Health and Safety at Work Act. Training will be bilingual (Danish/Greenlandic), include hands-on drills, and be integrated into onboarding via the digital shift calendar. Completion will be tracked and required before any operational activity.

Ensure all documentation and systems are ready for regulatory review by Nuuk Municipality. This includes verifying that safety training records, liability waivers, incident logs, and bilingual documentation are complete and accessible. Conduct a mock compliance check with the Katuaq Cultural Centre Oversight Committee to identify gaps and ensure alignment with Building Code Compliance Certificate and Fire Safety Certification requirements.

task_children.description: Implement 'Thermal Curfew' Protocol for Peak Winter Use

extra_fields.detailed_description: Collaborate with instructors and volunteers to design a clear, enforceable 'thermal curfew' protocol for peak winter use. Conduct a joint workshop to gather input on timing, enforcement methods, and user concerns. Ensure the policy aligns with safety standards and operational needs while minimizing disruption to course schedules.

Implement a phased pilot of the thermal curfew protocol during winter months using real-time IoT sensor alerts. Monitor compliance, collect feedback from users, and adjust alert thresholds and communication strategies based on actual usage patterns and environmental data.

Embed the thermal curfew into routine operations by updating digital dashboards, displaying bilingual signage at entry points, and including a mandatory orientation session before each winter session. Ensure all staff understand enforcement procedures and are trained to respond to alerts.

task_children.description: Develop Rotating Instructor Schedule with Cross-Training Plan

extra_fields.detailed_description: Create a rotating instructor schedule that ensures consistent course coverage by assigning overlapping teaching hours and built-in backup periods. Include mandatory cross-training modules for all instructors in at least two core skill areas (hand-building and wheel-throwing) to maintain operational resilience. Use the digital shift calendar to coordinate availability, flag conflicts early, and ensure all instructors confirm their schedules at least 30 days in advance.

Design and deliver a structured cross-training program where each part-time instructor is trained in at least two core competencies—hand-building and wheel-throwing—by experienced peers. Track progress via the real-time dashboard, conduct bi-weekly check-ins to address gaps, and require certification before independent teaching eligibility. Ensure all training materials are available in both Danish and Greenlandic.

Verify that all instructors have completed cross-training, signed liability waivers, and passed safety certification prior to any session. Confirm 100% staff availability through the digital shift calendar with no unassigned time slots. Conduct a dry-run simulation of a full week’s programming to test coverage under absences, using backup instructors from the rotating roster to fill gaps without disruption.

task_children.description: Launch Peer Mentorship Program for Senior-Junior Instructors

extra_fields.detailed_description: Define the framework for the peer mentorship program, including pairing criteria, duration of mentorship cycles, and expected outcomes. Develop a standardized toolkit with discussion guides, feedback forms, and milestone checklists to ensure consistency across all mentor-mentee relationships.

Recruit senior instructors and experienced local artists to serve as mentors. Conduct interviews to assess suitability, confirm availability, and sign formal agreements outlining roles, responsibilities, and compensation (if applicable). Ensure all participants receive initial orientation on cultural protocols and program expectations.

Begin a trial mentorship program with 3–5 junior instructors or apprentices. Facilitate initial pairings, schedule regular check-ins, and collect real-time feedback using digital tools. Use this pilot to refine the structure, address challenges, and validate the effectiveness of the program before full rollout.

After the pilot, conduct a comprehensive review of participant satisfaction, skill development progress, and program impact. Gather input from mentors, mentees, and the advisory council. Finalize improvements and expand the program to include all new instructors, ensuring integration into the broader teaching fellowship model.

task_children.description: Redesign Volunteer Instructor Program as Community Teaching Fellowship

extra_fields.detailed_description: Create a formal document outlining the roles, responsibilities, benefits, and expectations for the Community Teaching Fellowship. Include recognition pathways, skill credits, and structured onboarding procedures to ensure clarity and inclusivity. The charter must be co-developed with community leaders and approved by the advisory council.

Develop a comprehensive 12-hour certification program covering safety protocols, cultural sensitivity, teaching fundamentals, and emergency response. Use tools like Canva and Google Forms to create interactive modules. Include role-playing scenarios to simulate real-world challenges and ensure all content is available in both Danish and Greenlandic.

Build a digital onboarding kit with bilingual (Danish/Greenlandic) materials, including welcome guides, liability waiver templates, and training schedules. Test the system with a group of retired artists through a dry-run session to validate usability and gather feedback before full rollout.

Finalize and execute a $500,000 general liability insurance policy for volunteers. Develop and distribute legally compliant liability waivers that are signed by all participants. Ensure all documentation is bilingual and stored securely in a centralized digital system with automated tracking.

Roll out the fully operational 'Community Teaching Fellowship' by conducting the first live training session via Zoom. Use badge.io to issue digital badges upon completion. Track participation, certifications, and waiver submissions in a shared Notion dashboard to confirm 100% compliance before any instructor-led sessions begin.

task_children.description: Implement Digital Shift Calendar and Real-Time Tracking Dashboard

extra_fields.detailed_description: Configure a reliable cloud-based scheduling system using Google Workspace and Airtable to support real-time collaboration. This includes setting up user accounts, defining access roles, creating templates for shift calendars, and integrating with existing project management tools. Ensure the platform is accessible offline and supports bilingual (Danish/Greenlandic) interfaces.
resources_needed: Digital shift calendar software (Google Workspace + Airtable)

extra_fields.detailed_description: Create a standardized onboarding process for instructors and volunteers using Canva and Google Forms. Include multilingual forms for consent, training schedules, and safety protocols. Integrate automated reminders and progress tracking into the digital dashboard to ensure timely completion of all onboarding steps.

Conduct a pilot test with one instructor and two volunteers to validate the functionality of the digital shift calendar and real-time tracking dashboard. Identify bugs, usability issues, and communication gaps. Gather feedback through structured interviews and refine the system before full rollout.

Deliver hands-on training sessions for all staff and instructors on how to use the digital shift calendar and real-time tracking dashboard. Cover navigation, scheduling, conflict resolution, and data entry. Provide bilingual training materials and conduct follow-up check-ins to reinforce learning.

task_children.description: Roll out system to full team

extra_fields.detailed_description: Deploy the digital shift calendar and real-time tracking dashboard to all staff and instructors. Monitor initial usage for at least one week, address any technical or procedural issues promptly, and confirm that all users are actively engaged and compliant with the new system.

task_children.description: Conduct Monthly Safety Drills with Full Staff Participation

extra_fields.detailed_description: Organize and conduct monthly safety drills involving all staff to reinforce emergency protocols, team readiness, and compliance with operational procedures. Ensure drills are held on the first Friday of each month, integrated into the rotating instructor schedule, and include real-time feedback collection for continuous improvement.

task_children.description: Integrate Drill Participation in Performance Reviews

extra_fields.detailed_description: Embed participation in monthly safety drills as a formal component of staff performance evaluations. Use the digital shift calendar to track attendance and ensure accountability by linking drill completion to performance metrics and professional development records.

task_children.description: Conduct Drill Orientation Before Each Session

extra_fields.detailed_description: Deliver a brief orientation session before every monthly safety drill to remind participants of protocols, roles, and objectives. Use bilingual (Danish/Greenlandic) materials and display clear signage at entry points to reinforce awareness and reduce confusion during drills.

task_children.description: Form Advisory Council with Elders and Local Artists

extra_fields.detailed_description: Establish a formal advisory council comprising elders and local artists to guide cultural content, ensure community ownership, and provide ongoing feedback. This includes scheduling initial meetings, defining roles and responsibilities, and creating a communication protocol for regular engagement.
resources_needed: Digital Collaboration Platform (e.g., Notion or Zoom)

extra_fields.detailed_description: Collaboratively draft the Community Intellectual Property Rights (CIPR) framework through participatory workshops with elders, artists, and legal advisors. Define rules for access, consent, representation, and co-creation of traditional motifs and narratives, ensuring alignment with Inuit cultural values and Greenlandic legal standards.

Submit the finalized CIPR framework to the Inuit Cultural Heritage Council for official endorsement. Secure written consent from elders and artists on all culturally sensitive content, including approved and restricted motifs, and document all agreements in both Danish and Greenlandic.

task_children.description: Finalize and Obtain Approval of Community Intellectual Property Rights (CIPR) Framework

extra_fields.detailed_description: Develop a preliminary version of the Community Intellectual Property Rights (CIPR) framework through collaborative workshops with elders, local artists, and legal advisors. Define core principles such as consent protocols, access tiers, and cultural ownership boundaries. Ensure all documentation is drafted in both Danish and Greenlandic to support inclusive participation.
resources_needed: Notion or similar digital collaboration tool

extra_fields.detailed_description: Host a virtual or in-person workshop with elders and local artists to review the draft CIPR framework. Gather feedback on content sensitivity, representation accuracy, and community alignment. Use structured discussion guides and recording tools to document consensus and dissent, ensuring all input is formally captured.
resources_needed: Elders and Local Artists (advisory council members)
Facilitator trained in Indigenous consultation methods

extra_fields.detailed_description: Refine the CIPR framework based on feedback from the cultural review workshop. Finalize templates for written consent forms, approval workflows, and tiered access rules. Ensure all documents are legally sound, culturally respectful, and compliant with Greenlandic IP and labor laws.
resources_needed: Digital form platform (e.g., Notion, Google Forms)

extra_fields.detailed_description: Submit the finalized CIPR framework to the Inuit Cultural Heritage Council for formal approval. Present findings, consent records, and community feedback. Secure a signed agreement and official list of approved and restricted motifs before any public-facing cultural programming begins.
resources_needed: Signed agreement template and notarization services

task_children.description: Co-Host Quarterly Exhibitions with Katuaq Cultural Centre

extra_fields.detailed_description: Develop and secure formal approval of a Community Intellectual Property Rights (CIPR) framework with the Inuit Cultural Heritage Council. This includes drafting principles, creating consent templates in both Danish and Greenlandic, establishing a decision-making hierarchy for cultural content, and obtaining written agreement on approved and restricted motifs. The framework must ensure community sovereignty over traditional narratives and prevent cultural appropriation.

Obtain written consent from elders and local artists for all traditional motifs, stories, and cultural representations to be used in courses, exhibitions, and digital content. Conduct virtual or in-person consultations using culturally appropriate methods, record feedback, and document approvals with clear signatures or digital attestations in both Danish and Greenlandic.
resources_needed: Recording devices and secure digital storage

extra_fields.detailed_description: Design and implement a standardized, transparent workflow for reviewing and approving cultural content before public use. Define roles (e.g., advisory council, legal review, safety officer), set clear timelines, and integrate the process into the project’s digital dashboard. Ensure all submissions are tracked, reviewed, and documented with version control.

Embed the finalized CIPR framework into beginner courses by co-designing modules with elders and artists. Ensure all course content involving traditional forms or narratives is pre-approved, includes proper attribution, and reflects community input. Train instructors on cultural protocols and compliance requirements.

task_children.description: Integrate Traditional Forms and Narratives into Beginner Courses

extra_fields.detailed_description: Collaborate with elders and local artists through the advisory council to review and approve traditional forms, narratives, and motifs for inclusion in beginner courses. Ensure all content aligns with community values and cultural protocols, with written consent obtained for each element before integration.

Design beginner course modules that integrate approved traditional content into accessible, age-appropriate lessons. Include hands-on activities, storytelling components, and safety guidelines, ensuring alignment with both educational standards and cultural integrity.
resources_needed: Digital course builder (e.g., Notion or Google Docs)

extra_fields.detailed_description: Host a trial session with a small group of community members, including youth and elders, to test the course structure, content clarity, and cultural sensitivity. Gather feedback using bilingual surveys and adjust the curriculum based on input.

Compile feedback from the pilot session, revise course materials accordingly, and obtain final sign-off from the advisory council. Ensure all changes are documented and version-controlled for transparency and future reference.

Deliver a mandatory training session for all instructors on the cultural context, ethical use of traditional content, and teaching protocols. Use role-playing scenarios and real examples to reinforce understanding and ensure consistent delivery across sessions.

task_children.description: Ensure All Communications and Signage Are Bilingual (Danish/Greenlandic)

extra_fields.detailed_description: Develop and finalize all physical and digital signage in both Danish and Greenlandic, ensuring linguistic accuracy and cultural appropriateness. This includes safety warnings, course instructions, facility maps, and exhibition labels. Collaborate with a certified Greenlandic language specialist and a local cultural liaison to review each piece before production.

Conduct a thorough review of all signage content with the advisory council of elders and local artists to ensure no cultural misrepresentation or offense. Address feedback promptly and incorporate revisions into final designs. Maintain a documented approval trail for compliance and transparency.

Manufacture and install all finalized signage throughout the workshop, including entry points, kiln zones, drying areas, and exhibition spaces. Ensure placement adheres to safety standards and visibility requirements. Use weather-resistant materials suitable for Arctic conditions.

Confirm that all signage meets regulatory requirements under the Greenlander Health and Safety at Work Act and bilingual documentation mandates. Submit final installations to the National Labour Inspectorate (Greenland) for formal verification and obtain written confirmation of compliance.

Conduct a mandatory training session for all instructors and staff on the meaning, location, and purpose of each signage element. Include real-time scenarios where staff must interpret signs during simulated emergencies or visitor inquiries. Track attendance and comprehension via digital quiz.

task_children.description: Conduct Material Co-Creation Working Group Trials with Locally Sourced Minerals

extra_fields.detailed_description: Identify and secure access to viable local mineral sources such as glacial sand and volcanic ash through partnerships with Greenlandic geologists, mining cooperatives, and community leaders. Conduct initial feasibility assessments and obtain pre-approval from the advisory council for material sourcing.

Create 10 small batches of blended clay using varying ratios (70% imported clay, 30% local minerals). Document each batch with precise measurements and label them for tracking. Store samples in controlled conditions to prevent contamination or degradation.

Fire each blended clay batch at standard temperatures (1000°C–1200°C) using modular kilns. Monitor and record shrinkage rate, weight loss, structural integrity, and cracking incidence post-firing. Compare results against baseline imported clay performance.

Host a joint review session with elders and local artisans to interpret test results. Present findings in both Danish and Greenlandic, allowing for open discussion on authenticity, usability, and cultural appropriateness of the blended materials. Record consensus or concerns in bilingual minutes.

Compile all data—including technical measurements, firing outcomes, and cultural feedback—into a joint technical-oral report. Include recommendations for approved ratios, forbidden materials, and next steps for full-scale production. Obtain unanimous approval from the Material Co-Creation Working Group before publication.

task_children.description: Analyze Firing Behavior and Compatibility of Blended Clays

extra_fields.detailed_description: Conduct controlled firing tests on 10 batches of blended clay (70% imported, 30% local minerals) at standard temperatures (1000°C–1200°C) to evaluate shrinkage rate, weight loss, structural integrity, and cracking incidence. Record results in bilingual field notebooks (Danish/Greenlandic) and analyze for consistency with traditional ceramic standards.

Perform chemical and thermal analysis of locally sourced glacial sand and volcanic ash to assess compatibility with standard kiln processes. Compare results against known ceramic benchmarks from Køge Keramik and evaluate potential for long-term durability and aesthetic consistency.

Host a joint review session with elders and local artisans to interpret test results, assess material authenticity, and provide cultural feedback. Document consensus or concerns regarding the use of blended materials in community-led projects and co-creation initiatives.
resources_needed: Elders and artisans from advisory council

extra_fields.detailed_description: Compile test data, expert analysis, and community feedback into a formal report. Obtain unanimous approval from the Material Co-Creation Working Group and update the CIPR framework to include approved materials and usage guidelines.

task_children.description: Publish Joint Technical-Oral Report on Trial Results

extra_fields.detailed_description: Perform controlled tests on blended clay samples using varying ratios of imported clay and locally sourced glacial sand or volcanic ash. Fire samples at standard temperatures (1000°C–1200°C) to evaluate shrinkage rate, weight loss, structural integrity, and firing stability. Record results in bilingual field notebooks (Danish/Greenlandic) for accurate documentation.

Organize a joint review session with elders and local artisans to interpret trial results, assess material authenticity, and provide cultural insights. Use the feedback to determine whether the blended materials align with traditional practices and community values.

Compile all trial data, observations, and cultural feedback into a comprehensive report. Present the findings to the Material Co-Creation Working Group for consensus approval. Ensure unanimous agreement before any new materials are used in production.

task_children.description: Establish Partnership with Greenlandic Artisan Collectives for Material Sourcing

extra_fields.detailed_description: Establish formal agreements with local geologists and mining cooperatives to identify and secure access to viable glacial sand and volcanic ash sources in Greenland. Conduct initial site assessments and pre-negotiate terms for sustainable, community-approved extraction.

Test the feasibility of transporting small-scale mineral samples from remote Greenlandic regions via emergency air freight. Validate transit times, customs clearance efficiency, and packaging integrity under Arctic conditions.

Host a participatory workshop with elders, artisans, and local collectives to co-develop ethical and culturally respectful sourcing protocols. Define roles, responsibilities, consent mechanisms, and benefit-sharing models for material collection.

Draft and sign bilingual (Danish/Greenlandic) contracts with selected artisan collectives, including clauses on intellectual property, fair compensation, and long-term collaboration. Ensure all agreements are reviewed by legal counsel and approved by the advisory council.

Confirm end-to-end readiness of the local material supply chain by simulating a full procurement cycle: from extraction authorization to delivery at the workshop. Identify bottlenecks and refine processes before full-scale sourcing begins.

task_children.description: Begin Procurement of Locally Adapted Materials Only After Approval

extra_fields.detailed_description: Secure and confirm agreements with Greenlandic artisan collectives for the supply of locally sourced minerals, ensuring all partnerships are formalized with bilingual (Danish/Greenlandic) contracts. Verify that intellectual property rights and compensation terms are clearly defined and approved by the advisory council before any procurement begins.

Conduct laboratory and field testing of blended clay samples (70% imported, 30% local) to assess shrinkage rate, firing stability, and structural integrity at standard kiln temperatures (1000°C–1200°C). Record results in bilingual field notebooks and share findings with the Materials Scientist and Cultural Liaison for technical and cultural review.

Present trial results and proposed material use to the Material Co-Creation Working Group and elders for consensus. Ensure unanimous approval is documented through signed consent forms and a joint technical-oral report that integrates scientific data with oral history and cultural feedback.

task_children.description: Launch Themed Winter Programs (e.g., 'Winter Pottery Nights')

extra_fields.detailed_description: Develop engaging, culturally relevant themes for 'Winter Pottery Nights' that align with local traditions and seasonal activities. Collaborate with elders and artists to ensure authenticity and community resonance. Finalize themes by mid-February 2026 to allow time for promotional planning.

Create a rotating schedule of evening and weekend sessions to accommodate diverse participant availability, including working adults and families. Use the digital shift calendar to coordinate instructor availability and avoid conflicts. Ensure at least three session slots per week are available during winter months.

Execute a multi-channel outreach campaign using schools, radio stations, social media in Greenlandic, and community bulletin boards to promote 'Winter Pottery Nights'. Include incentives such as free materials or raffle prizes to boost initial attendance. Track response rates via bi-monthly surveys.

Ensure the workshop space is equipped for winter use with adequate heating, lighting, seating, and safety signage. Conduct a pre-event safety check with the Safety Officer and verify all emergency exits and fire equipment are accessible. Install temporary insulation if needed for thermal comfort.

task_children.description: Conduct Pilot Test with Small Group

extra_fields.detailed_description: Host a trial run of the first 'Winter Pottery Night' with a small group of 10–15 community members to test logistics, content flow, and participant feedback. Gather insights on timing, theme relevance, and experience quality. Use findings to refine the full-scale launch plan before August 1, 2026.

task_children.description: Design Micro-Courses for Tourists During Summer Peaks

extra_fields.detailed_description: Develop modular, short-duration micro-courses (1–2 hours) tailored for tourists during summer peaks. Focus on beginner-friendly experiences that highlight local clay traditions and techniques. Collaborate with instructors to ensure content is engaging, culturally respectful, and scalable. Finalize course structure, materials list, and session flow before pilot testing.

Establish formal agreements with at least three hotel partners to co-promote tourist experience packages. Define package inclusions (e.g., workshop duration, materials, guided tour), pricing tiers, and distribution channels. Align contract terms with seasonal demand patterns and ensure clear communication protocols for booking and guest management.

Integrate a flexible, real-time online booking system capable of handling dynamic pricing, capacity alerts, and multi-language support. Ensure the platform supports micro-course scheduling, instant confirmation, and automated reminders. Test with a small group of users to validate usability and reliability before full launch.

Run a trial of micro-courses with small groups of tourists during early summer to gather feedback on content, timing, logistics, and overall experience. Use insights to refine course delivery, adjust staffing needs, and optimize the booking system. Document outcomes and share results with stakeholders.

Analyze attendance and utilization metrics from the pilot phase to determine optimal course frequency, group size, and staffing levels. Adjust programming and marketing strategies accordingly to meet anticipated summer demand while maintaining quality and sustainability. Prepare for full-scale rollout by mid-summer.

Create themed, engaging winter programs such as 'Winter Pottery Nights' tailored to Nuuk's seasonal rhythms. Focus on flexible scheduling (evening/weekend slots), cultural relevance, and community appeal. Incorporate incentives like free materials or raffles to boost initial participation. Align with local traditions and ensure accessibility for elders and youth.

Run small-scale pilot sessions of the designed winter programs with a diverse group of participants including residents, elders, and youth. Gather real-time feedback on content, timing, and engagement. Use insights to refine program structure, themes, and delivery methods before full launch.

Establish formal agreements with at least three local hotels and tourism providers to co-promote and distribute the winter pottery experience packages. Ensure alignment on branding, pricing, and booking logistics. Use joint marketing materials and exclusive access perks to drive tourist interest.

Use historical attendance data from soft-launch events and real-time tracking dashboards to forecast winter demand. Adjust program capacity and staffing levels accordingly. Implement monthly review cycles with stakeholders to adapt programming based on actual utilization and feedback.

Conduct a comprehensive evaluation of winter program performance after the first season. Measure attendance, participant satisfaction, revenue generation, and community impact. Publish findings in a public report and use results to inform future seasonal programming strategies.

task_children.description: Align Programming with Seasonal Demand Patterns Using Forecast Data

extra_fields.detailed_description: Collect and analyze historical attendance data from similar cultural programs in Nuuk, focusing on winter and summer trends. Use surveys and community feedback to identify peak and off-peak periods. Develop a baseline forecast model for program utilization across seasons.

Align course offerings and event schedules with seasonal demand forecasts. Design winter-specific programs (e.g., 'Winter Pottery Nights') and summer micro-courses to match expected participation levels. Ensure staffing and material availability are synchronized with these cycles.

Establish a process to adjust session frequency, class size, and instructor assignments based on real-time utilization data. Use IoT sensor outputs and booking system metrics to trigger automatic alerts when demand exceeds or falls below thresholds.

task_children.description: Evaluate Attendance and Utilization Metrics Monthly

extra_fields.detailed_description: Gather real-time attendance and utilization metrics from IoT sensors, digital dashboards, and user feedback systems to assess program performance. Focus on winter and summer participation trends, session occupancy rates, and demographic breakdowns. Ensure data is collected consistently across all programs and stored in a centralized, bilingual (Danish/Greenlandic) database for analysis.
resources_needed: Digital shift calendar and real-time tracking dashboard

extra_fields.detailed_description: Use historical and real-time data to identify seasonal demand fluctuations in workshop usage, particularly during peak summer tourism and winter months. Apply statistical modeling to forecast future attendance based on past trends, weather conditions, and community engagement levels. Generate visual reports showing utilization gaps and overcapacity risks.

Revise course schedules, class sizes, and event offerings based on the insights from attendance and utilization analysis. Implement targeted adjustments such as increasing winter programming capacity, launching micro-courses during summer peaks, or reconfiguring open-studio hours to match demand. Ensure changes are communicated clearly through bilingual channels and aligned with stakeholder feedback loops.

Conduct a formal quarterly review of attendance and utilization data with key stakeholders including the Clay Workshop Manager, logistics coordinator, finance liaison, and safety officer. Evaluate the effectiveness of current programming adjustments, identify emerging trends, and update forecasting models. Document decisions and actions taken to ensure transparency and continuous improvement.

Develop clear, tiered membership levels for the 'Clay Pass' with distinct benefits (e.g., basic access, priority booking, exclusive events) based on resident feedback and budget constraints. Include pricing structures aligned with local income levels and ensure all tiers are accessible to diverse community members.

Conduct focus groups with local residents to test proposed pricing tiers and benefits, gather feedback on perceived value, and refine the offering before launch. Use real-time survey data to adjust tiers and messaging to maximize uptake and inclusivity.

Create a welcoming, multilingual onboarding process for new 'Clay Pass' members, including digital sign-up, orientation video, welcome kit, and access to introductory workshops. Ensure materials are available in both Danish and Greenlandic and reflect cultural sensitivity.

Implement the 'Clay Pass' membership tiers into the online booking platform with automated access controls, tier-specific privileges, and real-time availability tracking. Conduct usability testing with diverse users to ensure clarity and accessibility.

Roll out a pilot version of the 'Clay Pass' with flexible access and limited benefits to test demand, collect user feedback, and identify operational bottlenecks. Monitor participation rates and adjust the model based on early insights before full launch.

task_children.description: Create 'Tourist Experience Package' Sold via Hotel Partnerships

extra_fields.detailed_description: Develop a structured, culturally immersive ceramic experience package tailored for summer tourists, including session duration (1–2 hours), materials included, instructor roles, and pricing tiers. Collaborate with hotel partners to align with their booking systems and promotional calendars. Ensure all content reflects local traditions and is co-approved by the advisory council.

Negotiate and sign formal agreements with at least three hotel partners to distribute the tourist experience package. Define commission structures, booking caps, promotional responsibilities, and cancellation policies. Ensure contracts are bilingual (Danish/Greenlandic) and compliant with Greenlandic commercial laws.

Implement the tourist experience package into the online booking platform with tiered pricing, real-time availability tracking, and automated confirmation emails. Test the integration with sample bookings to ensure seamless user experience across devices and languages. Validate that capacity alerts trigger when limits are reached.

Host a small-scale trial of the tourist experience package with a group of 10–15 guests during early August 2026. Gather feedback via post-session surveys and real-time observations. Use insights to refine session flow, materials, and staff training before full rollout.

Distribute branded promotional materials (brochures, digital banners, QR codes) to partner hotels. Train front-desk staff on how to explain and book the experience. Coordinate joint marketing campaigns (e.g., social media posts, welcome kits) to drive visibility and bookings during peak season.

task_children.description: Implement Pay-What-You-Can Model for Winter Months

extra_fields.detailed_description: Test the pay-what-you-can pricing model with a small group of local residents during a winter session to evaluate uptake, perceived value, and revenue impact. Gather feedback on pricing thresholds and messaging through post-session surveys and informal discussions.

task_children.description: Integrate Flexible Pricing into Booking System

extra_fields.detailed_description: Configure the online booking platform to support tiered and flexible pricing options, including real-time capacity alerts and automated payment processing. Ensure the system is accessible in both Danish and Greenlandic and includes clear instructions for users.

Conduct a mandatory training session for all instructors and front-desk staff on how to apply the pay-what-you-can model consistently, including guidelines for handling payments, managing exceptions, and communicating the policy clearly to participants.

Track key performance indicators such as session attendance, average contribution per participant, and overall revenue during the pilot phase. Use real-time dashboard data to identify trends and adjust the model based on early insights.

task_children.description: Integrate Tiered Pricing into Online Booking System

extra_fields.detailed_description: Implement a tiered pricing model into the online booking system to support flexible access for residents and tourists. This includes configuring price tiers based on session type, duration, and user category (e.g., resident, tourist, youth). Ensure compatibility with payment gateways, test functionality across devices, and validate that real-time capacity alerts are triggered when limits are reached. Finalize integration before soft launch.

Conduct end-to-end testing of the online booking system with all pricing tiers, including edge cases such as overbooking, expired sessions, and multi-user reservations. Use a sandbox environment to simulate real-world usage patterns. Validate that automated reminders, capacity alerts, and payment confirmations function correctly. Document issues and coordinate fixes with the tech team.

Provide comprehensive training to all instructors and front-desk staff on how to explain and apply the tiered pricing model, including the pay-what-you-can option during winter months. Deliver bilingual training materials, conduct role-playing scenarios, and verify understanding through quizzes. Ensure all staff can confidently handle customer inquiries and resolve pricing-related disputes.

Monitor initial booking data after implementation to assess revenue predictability. Analyze trends in participation by user group, time of day, and season. Use dashboard metrics to identify underperforming tiers or unexpected demand spikes. Adjust pricing models quarterly based on performance data and stakeholder feedback.

task_children.description: Monitor Revenue Predictability and Adjust Models Quarterly

extra_fields.detailed_description: Set up automated data collection from IoT sensors and digital dashboards to ensure continuous monitoring of revenue, attendance, and utilization metrics. Integrate weekly export schedules into the system to provide consistent, real-time insights for decision-making.
resources_needed: Digital dashboard platform (e.g., Airtable or Notion)

extra_fields.detailed_description: Designate a rotating role among instructors to oversee revenue performance on a monthly basis. This individual will review aggregated data, identify trends, and prepare reports for stakeholder meetings, ensuring consistent accountability and transparency.

Hold structured quarterly meetings with key stakeholders—including the Clay Workshop Manager, Logistics Coordinator, and Finance Liaison—to evaluate performance data, discuss challenges, and approve adjustments to pricing models and programming based on actual utilization and feedback.
resources_needed: Stakeholder list (manager, logistics coordinator, finance liaison)

task_children.description: Host First 'Community Pottery Day' with Public Installation Project

extra_fields.detailed_description: Define event scope, select location, draft timeline, and coordinate with local artists and elders for content approval. Include logistics for public installation project, ensure bilingual communication, and schedule pre-event promotion through schools, radio, and social media.

Procure clay, tools, and installation materials in advance; confirm availability of drying racks and ventilation systems. Ensure all items are delivered on time with buffer stock for weather-related delays, and verify compatibility with the workshop’s thermal and safety standards.

Design and build a collaborative public artwork using community-contributed pieces. Conduct on-site assembly with volunteers, ensure structural safety, and integrate cultural motifs approved by the advisory council. Document process with photos and videos for digital archive.

Run the 'Community Pottery Day' with guided activities, live demonstrations, and interactive stations. Facilitate elder-led storytelling sessions, distribute free materials to participants, and collect real-time feedback via bilingual surveys. Broadcast event highlights on local media and social platforms.

Review attendance data, survey responses, and incident logs post-event. Compile a joint report with elders and artists summarizing community impact, lessons learned, and recommendations for future co-creation events. Upload final artworks and documentation to public digital platform and physical display locations.

task_children.description: Launch Youth Apprenticeship Track with School Partnerships

extra_fields.detailed_description: Establish formal agreements with local schools to integrate the youth apprenticeship track into their curricula. This includes identifying key stakeholders (principals, teachers, guidance counselors), scheduling initial meetings, drafting partnership memorandums in both Danish and Greenlandic, and aligning program timelines with academic calendars to ensure seamless participation.

Design a structured, culturally grounded curriculum for youth apprentices that blends technical ceramic skills with intergenerational storytelling and community co-creation. The curriculum must include learning modules on hand-building, wheel-throwing, material sourcing, safety protocols, and cultural context, all delivered in bilingual (Danish/Greenlandic) materials with visual aids and hands-on activities.

Create a rotating peer mentorship system pairing experienced instructors with apprentices, ensuring consistent guidance and skill transfer. Define clear roles, expectations, and evaluation criteria for mentors and mentees. Conduct a pilot mentorship cycle with a small group of apprentices to test structure, timing, and feedback mechanisms before full rollout.

Run a trial 6-week apprenticeship program with 10–12 youth participants from local schools. Deliver the curriculum in a hybrid format (in-person at workshop + virtual check-ins), collect real-time feedback via bi-weekly surveys, and assess skill progression through milestone projects. Use results to refine the program before scaling.
resources_needed: IoT monitoring sensors for activity tracking

extra_fields.detailed_description: Analyze data from the pilot cohort—including attendance, skill development, participant satisfaction, and instructor feedback—to identify strengths and gaps. Adjust the curriculum, mentorship model, and logistics accordingly. Finalize a scalable framework for annual recruitment and launch the full youth apprenticeship track by September 2026.

task_children.description: Introduce AI-Assisted Design Tools with Cultural Context Guidance

extra_fields.detailed_description: Collaborate with elders and local artists to develop culturally relevant training datasets for AI-assisted design tools. Pilot the tool with a small group of instructors, ensuring alignment with traditional practices and community values. Address technical integration challenges and provide hands-on workshops to build trust and familiarity among staff.

Embed cultural narratives and design principles into the AI tool's framework through co-design sessions. Ensure all AI-generated suggestions respect sacred motifs and oral histories. Validate content with the advisory council before full deployment to prevent misrepresentation.

Conduct a controlled pilot of the AI-assisted design tool with four part-time instructors using real project scenarios. Gather feedback on usability, cultural appropriateness, and technical performance. Refine the tool based on observed challenges and instructor input.

Develop a rollout strategy for the AI-assisted design tool, including training modules, access protocols, and integration with existing workflows. Secure formal approval from the advisory council and safety officer. Prepare documentation in both Danish and Greenlandic for all users.

task_children.description: Establish Feedback Loops with Community through Bi-Monthly Surveys

extra_fields.detailed_description: Gather feedback from local residents, elders, and artists through bi-monthly surveys to assess engagement, identify cultural sensitivities, and evaluate the impact of co-created content. Use bilingual (Danish/Greenlandic) digital and printed forms distributed via schools, community centers, and social media. Incentivize participation with small rewards like free workshop vouchers to improve response rates.
resources_needed: Digital survey platform (e.g., Google Forms, Notion)

extra_fields.detailed_description: Organize quarterly in-person or virtual workshops with community stakeholders—including elders, youth apprentices, and local artists—to review survey results, discuss concerns, and co-develop action plans. Ensure all sessions are facilitated by a cultural liaison, conducted in both Danish and Greenlandic, and documented with minutes and visual summaries for transparency.

Update course content, exhibition themes, and operational policies based on validated community input. Implement changes through iterative cycles: analyze feedback, prioritize actions, revise materials, pilot adjustments, and re-survey to measure improvement. Maintain a public dashboard showing how feedback led to real changes to build trust.

Create and distribute bi-monthly public reports summarizing survey findings, workshop outcomes, and implemented changes. Include data visualizations, quotes from community members, and clear timelines. Share via email newsletters, social media, Katuaq Cultural Centre bulletin boards, and school networks to ensure broad accessibility.

task_children.description: Publish Co-Created Artworks in Public Spaces and Digital Platforms

extra_fields.detailed_description: Establish a formal review process with the Inuit Cultural Heritage Council and advisory council to approve all co-created artworks for public display, ensuring cultural sensitivity and community consent. This includes verifying that no restricted motifs or narratives are included and confirming alignment with the CIPR framework.

Plan and coordinate the physical installation of co-created artworks in public spaces (e.g., Katuaq Cultural Centre, Nuuk Center) during favorable weather windows (spring/summer). Secure transport contracts with local providers, confirm weather-resilient mounting solutions, and ensure all materials are pre-approved by the advisory council.

Create and launch a bilingual (Danish/Greenlandic) digital archive of co-created artworks, including metadata such as artist names, cultural context, creation date, and approval status. Ensure the platform is accessible via mobile and desktop, supports multimedia, and integrates with the public dashboard for transparency.

Organize a public event to unveil the co-created artworks in both physical and digital formats. Invite elders, artists, youth apprentices, and residents to participate in guided tours, storytelling sessions, and feedback discussions. Use this opportunity to reinforce community ownership and celebrate cultural collaboration.

task_children.description: Ensure Regulatory Compliance and Stakeholder Engagement
Submit All Permit Applications in Danish and Greenlandic by April 20, 2026

extra_fields.detailed_description: Compile and verify all required documentation for Nuuk Municipality permits, including bilingual (Danish/Greenlandic) submissions. Ensure compliance with Building Code, Fire Safety, Environmental Protection, and Noise Control regulations. Prepare digital and physical copies for submission by April 20, 2026.

Perform an internal audit of all permit documents using checklists aligned with NFPA, Bygningsreglementet, and Greenlandic Health and Safety at Work Act. Engage the Katuaq Cultural Centre Oversight Committee for feedback on cultural and community-related aspects of the application.

Deliver all completed permit applications in both Danish and Greenlandic to Nuuk Municipality Planning Department by the deadline. Track submission confirmation and initiate follow-up protocol if no acknowledgment is received within 48 hours.

Schedule and conduct proactive check-ins with Nuuk Municipality planning officials and other regulatory bodies to monitor application status. Address any feedback or missing items promptly to avoid delays in approval timeline.

Develop a contingency plan for anticipated revisions based on past municipal review patterns. Maintain editable versions of all documents and assign a dedicated team member to manage updates and resubmissions if required.

task_children.description: Conduct Pre-Opening Thermal and Safety Audits with Certified Arctic Specialist

extra_fields.detailed_description: Perform a comprehensive pre-opening thermal audit of the facility using certified Arctic building standards. This includes assessing insulation integrity, thermal mass performance, and heat retention across all zones under simulated winter conditions (-20°C). The audit must validate that indoor temperatures remain within ±5°C of target parameters during peak occupancy (90%) and identify any necessary upgrades before fit-out completion.

Ensure all safety systems meet Greenlandic Health and Safety at Work Act and International Fire Safety Codes (NFPA 80, NFPA 101). Conduct on-site inspections of ventilation, fire suppression systems, emergency exits, and kiln operation protocols. Document findings and implement corrective actions prior to certification submission.

Compile and finalize all required documentation for Nuuk Municipality review, including bilingual (Danish/Greenlandic) versions of the Building Code Compliance Certificate application, Fire Safety Inspection Report, Environmental Protection Act Registration, and Noise Control Permit. Ensure all forms are signed, notarized, and submitted by April 20, 2026.
resources_needed: Digital filing system with version control

extra_fields.detailed_description: Coordinate with Nuuk Municipality Planning Department and Katuaq Cultural Centre Oversight Committee to schedule a formal review meeting one week before the final compliance audit. Present preliminary findings, address feedback, and confirm readiness for inspection. Use this opportunity to resolve any outstanding concerns proactively.

Assemble a complete audit readiness package containing all verified records: thermal load data, safety inspection reports, training logs, liability waivers, incident logs, and digital dashboards. Conduct a dry run with internal staff to simulate the audit process and ensure full compliance with all requirements before official submission.

task_children.description: Obtain Building Code Compliance Certificate and Fire Safety Certification

extra_fields.detailed_description: Gather and compile all required documents for regulatory compliance, including safety training records, liability waiver forms, incident logs, and certification proofs. Ensure all materials are bilingual (Danish/Greenlandic) and organized in a centralized digital repository with version control.
resources_needed: Centralized Digital Repository (e.g., Notion or Google Drive)

extra_fields.detailed_description: Perform a pre-submission review of all compliance records using checklists aligned with Greenlandic Health and Safety at Work Act and NFPA standards. Identify gaps and ensure all staff have completed mandatory training and signed waivers.

Deliver all compiled compliance documentation to Nuuk Municipality in both Danish and Greenlandic by the deadline of April 20, 2026. Include confirmation of receipt and initiate follow-up protocol for any missing items or feedback.

Respond promptly to any queries or deficiencies raised by Nuuk Municipality during the review process. Update records, re-submit corrected documents, and maintain a log of all communications and revisions.
Confirm official acceptance of all compliance records through written confirmation from Nuuk Municipality. Document final approval status and integrate it into the project’s readiness dashboard.

task_children.description: Schedule Compliance Audit with Nuuk Municipality One Week Before Opening

extra_fields.detailed_description: Set up a centralized digital platform to record and monitor safety training completion, liability waiver submissions, and incident logs. Ensure the system supports bilingual (Danish/Greenlandic) input, automated reminders for deadlines, and real-time access for the Safety Officer and project manager. Integrate with the digital shift calendar to flag overdue entries.
resources_needed: Digital tracking software (e.g., Notion or Airtable)

extra_fields.detailed_description: Create clear, step-by-step guidelines for staff on how to log safety training, submit liability waivers, and report incidents. Include templates in both Danish and Greenlandic, define roles and responsibilities, and specify retention periods. Distribute protocols via onboarding materials and post them in visible areas within the workshop.

task_children.description: Conduct Staff Training on Compliance Procedures

extra_fields.detailed_description: Organize mandatory training sessions for all instructors and volunteers on how to use the digital tracking system and follow documentation protocols. Use role-playing scenarios to reinforce accountability and include a quiz to verify understanding. Record attendance and completion status in the system.

Implement a routine audit process where the rotating compliance champion reviews records every two weeks. Check for completeness, accuracy, and timeliness of training logs, waivers, and incident reports. Address gaps immediately and escalate recurring issues to the project manager.

task_children.description: Integrate Records into Final Compliance Audit

extra_fields.detailed_description: Ensure all safety training, waiver, and incident records are compiled and verified before the final compliance audit with Nuuk Municipality. Confirm that the digital system has full audit trails, backup copies are stored securely, and all documents are available in both Danish and Greenlandic for review.

task_children.description: Maintain Records of Safety Training, Liability Waivers, and Incident Logs

extra_fields.detailed_description: Set up a centralized digital platform to log and monitor safety training completion, liability waiver submissions, and incident reports. Ensure the system supports bilingual (Danish/Greenlandic) input, automated reminders for upcoming deadlines, and real-time access for the Safety Officer and Clay Workshop Manager. Integrate with the existing digital shift calendar and conduct a pilot test with one instructor team.
resources_needed: Digital tracking software (e.g., Notion or Airtable)

extra_fields.detailed_description: Create standardized templates for safety training records, liability waivers, and incident logs in both Danish and Greenlandic. Include fields for date, participant name, role, training topic, signature, and notes. Validate templates with legal and cultural advisors to ensure compliance with Greenlandic labor and IP laws, and distribute them to all staff and volunteers.

Implement a mandatory compliance check during instructor and volunteer onboarding. Verify that each individual has completed safety training, signed a liability waiver, and submitted required documentation through the digital system. Flag incomplete records immediately and prevent participation in any session until resolved.

Schedule and execute bi-weekly audits of all safety, waiver, and incident records to ensure accuracy, completeness, and timeliness. Use the audit findings to identify gaps, provide feedback to staff, and update the tracking system as needed. Document audit results and share summaries with the Clay Workshop Manager and Safety Officer.

Integrate compliance status into the real-time dashboard used by the Clay Workshop Manager and Safety Officer. Display key metrics such as % of staff trained, % of waivers submitted, and number of open incident reports. Enable alerts for overdue items and allow instant access to full records during inspections or audits.
resources_needed: Digital dashboard (e.g., Airtable or Google Sheets)

task_children.description: Execute Pre-Opening Milestones and Final Readiness Checks
Secure Lease Agreement for 120–150 m² Space Near Katuaq

extra_fields.detailed_description: Negotiate and finalize the lease agreement for a 120–150 m² commercial space near Katuaq Cultural Centre, ensuring all terms align with project needs including use restrictions, rent escalation clauses, and access rights. Coordinate with a local real estate advisor to expedite legal review and secure bilingual (Danish/Greenlandic) documentation.

Perform a physical site inspection to verify space suitability, assess structural integrity, confirm access routes, and evaluate proximity to utilities and public transport. Document findings and compare against project requirements for ventilation, lighting, and workflow layout.

Obtain formal written approval from the landlord for the intended use of the space as a clay workshop, including compliance with commercial zoning regulations. Submit a detailed operational plan and safety overview to address any concerns.

Submit required documentation to Nuuk Municipality for official registration of the new commercial space, including proof of lease, business intent, and compliance with local planning and environmental standards. Track application status and respond promptly to feedback.

Verify that all pre-lease conditions are met, including finalizing utility connections, securing emergency access routes, and confirming availability of key personnel for handover. Conduct a dry-run walkthrough with the facility team to ensure readiness for fit-out phase.

task_children.description: Hire and Onboard Four Part-Time Instructors

extra_fields.detailed_description: Identify and attract four qualified part-time instructors with experience in hand-building and wheel-throwing. Conduct interviews, verify credentials, and assess cultural alignment with the project’s values. Prioritize candidates with local ties or Greenlandic language skills to support community integration.

Deliver a comprehensive onboarding program covering safety protocols, cultural content guidelines, teaching methodologies, and facility operations. Include mandatory training modules on Arctic-specific risks, liability waivers, and use of IoT monitoring systems. Ensure all instructors complete training at least two weeks before first course delivery.

Draft and execute formal employment agreements for all four part-time instructors. Include terms on compensation, scheduling flexibility, cross-training requirements, and compliance with safety and cultural policies. Ensure contracts are signed in both Danish and Greenlandic and stored securely in the digital records system.

Develop and implement a rotating instructor schedule using the digital shift calendar. Ensure each instructor is cross-trained in at least two core skill areas (hand-building, wheel-throwing) and has backup coverage during absences. Test the schedule with a dry-run session to validate coverage and avoid conflicts.

Confirm that all instructors have completed safety training, signed liability waivers, and passed a final readiness check. Conduct a pre-launch review with the Safety Officer and Project Manager to ensure full compliance. Document all verification steps for audit purposes.

task_children.description: Complete Fit-Out and Install Kilns, Worktables, Drying Racks, and Ventilation

extra_fields.detailed_description: Order and confirm delivery of kilns, worktables, drying racks, and ventilation systems with guaranteed Arctic shipping timelines. Prioritize modular, pre-fabricated components to reduce on-site assembly time. Verify supplier reliability and include contingency clauses for weather-related delays.

Coordinate installation dates with certified Arctic contractors during favorable weather windows (late spring/early summer). Conduct pre-installation site checks for structural readiness and ensure all equipment is delivered in sequence to avoid bottlenecks.

Perform detailed inspections of all equipment upon arrival to verify integrity, compliance with safety standards, and compatibility with the workshop layout. Document any defects or discrepancies immediately and initiate replacement or repair processes.

Execute installation of kilns, worktables, drying racks, and ventilation systems according to design specifications. Perform functional tests post-installation, including airflow verification, temperature control calibration, and structural stability checks.

Complete all remaining fit-out tasks including final cleaning, safety signage placement, and integration of IoT environmental monitoring sensors. Conduct a full walkthrough with the safety officer and project manager to confirm operational readiness.

task_children.description: Deliver First Course to Soft Launch Audience on August 1, 2026

extra_fields.detailed_description: Ensure all teaching sessions are covered by cross-trained instructors with backup availability. Develop a rotating schedule using the digital shift calendar, confirm instructor availability at least two weeks in advance, and assign backup instructors for each session to prevent cancellations due to absences.

Confirm that the 50 kg buffer stock of clay is physically present and stored properly before the soft launch. Conduct a visual and documentation check against delivery records and ensure it meets quality standards for immediate use.

Simulate the first course delivery one week before August 1, 2026, to test staffing coverage, equipment functionality, material availability, and logistics. Identify and resolve any issues related to scheduling, communication, or supply chain gaps.

task_children.description: Validate Liability Waivers and Training Completion

extra_fields.detailed_description: Ensure all instructors and volunteers have signed liability waivers and completed mandatory safety training. Verify documentation through the digital tracking system and confirm no outstanding items remain before session delivery.

Use IoT sensors to continuously monitor temperature, humidity, and CO₂ levels during the dry run and soft launch. Trigger alerts if thresholds are breached and verify that the 'thermal curfew' protocol is ready for activation.

task_children.description: Achieve 100% Session Coverage with No Cancellations Due to Supply or Staffing Issues

extra_fields.detailed_description: Ensure all instructors and volunteers complete mandatory safety training and cultural protocols at least two weeks before the soft launch. Verify attendance records and digital badge completion via the real-time tracking dashboard. Conduct a final review with the Safety Officer to confirm readiness.

Confirm that the 50 kg buffer stock of clay is physically present, stored properly, and verified as usable. Cross-check against shipment logs and IoT sensor data from the monitoring system to ensure no supply chain disruptions have occurred.

Implement a rotating backup instructor schedule using cross-trained staff. Use the digital shift calendar to auto-reassign sessions in case of last-minute absences. Conduct a dry-run simulation to test coverage and response time.

Run a full-scale mock session on August 1, simulating course delivery, equipment use, and emergency response. Test staffing coverage, material availability, and communication flow. Document any issues and resolve them before the actual soft launch.

Ensure 100% of staff and volunteers have signed liability waivers and that all documents are digitally logged and accessible. Confirm insurance coverage is active and linked to the project’s general liability policy.
